socrates.text: {"analysis":"The autotune got 0.6030 accuracy with a 16.1MB model (way under 150MB). The constraint wasn't the issue - the autotune just didn't find good enough hyperparameters in 5 minutes. Let me try: 1) longer autotune duration (10 min), 2) no model size constraint,

3) use character n-grams (minn/maxn) which help with generalization. I'll also try 'ova' loss which can help multi-class.","plan":"Run a new training script with longer autotune, no size constraint, and character n-gram support. Then quantize to fit under 150MB if needed.","commands":[{"keystrokes":"cat > /app/train4.py << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import fasttext
import os
import re

def clean_text(text):
    text = str(text).lower()
    text = text.replace('\n', ' ').

replace('\r', ' ')
    text = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\s]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

print('Loading data...')
train_df = pd.read_parquet('data/train-00000-of-00001.parquet')
test_df = pd.read_parquet('data/test-00000-of-00001.parquet')

print('Writing full train.txt...')
with open('/app/train_full.txt', 'w') as f:
    for _, row in train_df.iterrows():
        f.write(f'__label__{row["label"]} {clean_text(row["text"]

)}\n')

print('Writing test.txt...')
with open('/app/test.txt', 'w') as f:
    for _, row in test_df.iterrows():
        f.write(f'__label__{row["label"]} {clean_text(row["text"])}\n')

# Manual training with known good params for text classification
# Using character n-grams for better generalization
print('Training model with char n-grams...')
model = fasttext.train_supervised(
    input='/app/train_full.txt',
    lr=1.0,
    epoch=50,
    wordNgrams=2,
    dim=200,
    loss='ova',
    bucket=2000000,

minCount=1,
    minn=3,
    maxn=6,
    thread=4,
    verbose=2
)

print('\nEvaluating on test set...')
n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
print(f'Unquantized - Samples: {n}, Precision: {p:.4f}, Recall: {r:.4f}')

model.save_model('/app/model_unquant.bin')
size_mb = os.path.getsize('/app/model_unquant.bin') / (1024*1024)
print(f'Unquantized size: {size_mb:.1f} MB')

if size_mb > 150:
    print('\nQuantizing to

fit under 150MB...')
    model.quantize(input='/app/train_full.txt', retrain=True, epoch=10, lr=0.5, thread=4, dsub=2, qnorm=True)
    model.save_model('/app/model.bin')
    size_mb = os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') / (1024*1024)
    print(f'Quantized size: {size_mb:.1f} MB')
    
    n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
    print(f'Quantized - Samples: {n}, Precision: {p:.4f}, Recall: {r:.4f}')
else:
    os.rename('/app/model_unquant.bin', '/app/model.bin')

Then quantize to fit under 150MB if needed.","commands":[{"keystrokes":"cat > /app/train4.py << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import fasttext
import os
import re

def clean_text(text):
    text = str(text).lower()
    text = text.replace('\n', ' ').replace('\r', ' ')
    text = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\s]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

print('Loading data...')
train_df = pd.read_parquet('data/train-00000-of-00001.parquet')
test_df = pd.read_parquet('data/test-00000-of-00001.parquet')

print('Writing full train.txt...')
with open('/app/train_full.txt', 'w') as f:
    for _, row in train_df.iterrows():
        f.write(f'__label__{row["label"]} {clean_text(row["text"])}\n')

print('Writing test.txt...')
with open('/app/test.txt', 'w') as f:
    for _, row in test_df.iterrows():
        f.write(f'__label__{row["label"]} {clean_text(row["text"])}\n')

# Manual training with known good params for text classification
# Using character n-grams for better generalization
print('Training model with char n-grams...')
model = fasttext.train_supervised(
    input='/app/train_full.txt',
    lr=1.0,
    epoch=50,
    wordNgrams=2,
    dim=200,
    loss='ova',
    bucket=2000000,
    minCount=1,
    minn=3,
    maxn=6,
    thread=4,
    verbose=2
)

print('\nEvaluating on test set...')
n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
print(f'Unquantized - Samples: {n}, Precision: {p:.4f}, Recall: {r:.4f}')

model.save_model('/app/model_unquant.bin')
size_mb = os.path.getsize('/app/model_unquant.bin') / (1024*1024)
print(f'Unquantized size: {size_mb:.1f} MB')

if size_mb > 150:
    print('\nQuantizing to fit under 150MB...')
    model.quantize(input='/app/train_full.txt', retrain=True, epoch=10, lr=0.5, thread=4, dsub=2, qnorm=True)
    model.save_model('/app/model.bin')
    size_mb = os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') / (1024*1024)
    print(f'Quantized size: {size_mb:.1f} MB')
    
    n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
    print(f'Quantized - Samples: {n}, Precision: {p:.4f}, Recall: {r:.4f}')
else:
    os.rename('/app/model_unquant.bin', '/app/model.bin')
    print(f'Model saved as /app/model.bin ({size_mb:.1f} MB)')

print('\nDone!')

import pandas as pd
import fasttext
import os
import re

def clean_text(text):

    text = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\s]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

print('Loading data...')
train_df = pd.read_parquet('data/train-00000-of-00001.parquet')
test_df = pd.read_parquet('data/test-00000-of-00001.parquet')

print('Writing full train.txt...')
with open('/app/train_full.txt', 'w') as f:
    for _, row in train_df.iterrows():
        f.write(f'__label__{row["label"]} {clean_text(row["text"])}\n')

print('Writing test.txt...')
with open('/app/test.txt', 'w') as f:
    for _, row in test_df.iterrows():
        f.write(f'__label__{row["label"]} {clean_text(row["text"])}\n')

# Manual training with known good params for text classification
# Using character n-grams for better generalization
print('Training model with char n-grams...')
model = fasttext.train_supervised(
    input='/app/train_full.txt',
    lr=1.0,
    epoch=50,
    wordNgrams=2,
    dim=200,
    loss='ova',
    bucket=2000000,
    minCount=1,
    minn=3,
    maxn=6,
    thread=4,
    verbose=2
)

print('\nEvaluating on test set...')
n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
print(f'Unquantized - Samples: {n}, Precision: {p:.4f}, Recall: {r:.4f}')

model.save_model('/app/model_unquant.bin')
size_mb = os.path.getsize('/app/model_unquant.bin') / (1024*1024)
print(f'Unquantized size: {size_mb:.1f} MB')

if size_mb > 150:
    print('\nQuantizing to fit under 150MB...')
    model.quantize(input='/app/train_full.txt', retrain=True, epoch=10, lr=0.5, thread=4, dsub=2, qnorm=True)
    model.save_model('/app/model.bin')
    size_mb = os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') / (1024*1024)
    print(f'Quantized size: {size_mb:.1f} MB')
    
    n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
